name: Camden Firemen'S
description_short: Camden Firemen's Federal Credit Union is a small community credit union established in 1945, serving over 100 members with savings accounts, loans, and deposit products from its Moorestown, NJ location.
description_long: Camden Firemen's Federal Credit Union was established in 1945 and is headquartered in Moorestown, New Jersey. As a federally chartered credit union, it operates as a not-for-profit financial institution designed to serve members with a specific common bond. The credit union has maintained operations for nearly 80 years, serving a niche membership base in the New Jersey area. The institution offers a range of personal banking and lending products including savings accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), auto loans, personal loans, and mortgages. Members can access online banking and mobile apps for both iOS and Android devices, as well as online bill pay services. The credit union provides financial calculators and comparison tools to help members make informed decisions about rates and products. As a not-for-profit credit union, Camden Firemen's positions itself on offering competitive rates and lower fees compared to for-profit banks. The institution is small by design, with one physical branch location and no ATM network, which limits accessibility but reflects its focus on serving a specific membership community. The credit union maintains online banking availability and provides customer service through phone and in-person channels. The primary limitation of this institution is its extremely small size—with only 100+ members, $587,100 in assets, and a single branch with no ATM access, it offers minimal infrastructure and geographic convenience. The lack of ATMs and branches makes it unsuitable for members seeking widespread access. Additionally, as a membership-restricted credit union, not all consumers qualify to join, and the limited member base may affect service responsiveness and product development compared to larger institutions.
pros: Not-for-profit structure typically offers competitive rates and lower fees than commercial banks
Offers mobile banking apps for iOS and Android platforms
Provides online bill pay functionality for convenience
Auto loans available at competitive rates (9.07% for used vehicles)
Mortgage products available for home financing
Certificates of Deposit (CDs) offered for savings goals
Online banking portal accessible 24/7 when system is operational
cons: Only 1 physical branch with 0 ATMs, severely limiting cash access and service availability
Very small membership base (100+ members) may result in limited service responsiveness and product innovation
Membership restricted to specific groups with common bond, not open to general public
Limited infrastructure and scale compared to traditional banks or larger credit unions
Currently showing 'lobby currently closed' status, raising operational concerns
best_for: Existing members with the required common bond seeking basic banking and personal lending
Firefighters or related community members in the Camden, New Jersey area
Individuals prioritizing credit union membership for not-for-profit structure and competitive rates
diagnosis: Camden Firemen's Federal Credit Union is best suited for existing members who qualify for membership through the credit union's common bond requirement and who value not-for-profit banking with competitive rates. The critical caveat is the institution's extremely limited physical infrastructure—with only one branch and no ATMs—making it impractical for members requiring frequent in-person or cash access services.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
